metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2437
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.1: "N-WASP localizes to spines and functional, excitatory synapses in hippocampal neurons"
- Literal: "As shown in the higher magnification images, N-WASP puncta were in close apposition to the presynaptic marker SV2, suggesting it was in postsynaptic terminals (Fig. 1A). Consistent with this, N-WASP puncta almost completely merged with the postsynaptic marker PSD-95 (Fig. 1A), indicating that N-WASP localizes to the postsynaptic side of excitatory synapses.". Experimental description: Cell Culture and Transfection-Hippocampal low density cultures were prepared as described previously (18). Reference 18 is entitled: Rat Hippocampal Neurons in Low-density Culture (Banker, G., and Goslin, K., eds) pp. 339-370, MIT Press, Cambridge, MA

8/2/2018 Pim
- Species of overexpressed N-Wasp is not stated in the paper. Annotation was made based on the endogenous N-Wasp protein.
